title: Repository-native prompt compression with phase-local manifold scheduling
goal: Implement and evaluate a deterministic Prompt IR, phase-local activation frontier, invariant-hypergraph closure, model-tokenizer accounting, verified renderers, and adversarial benchmark so coding-agent prompts minimize token cost without weakening repository authority, exact commands, stop conditions, or evidence boundaries.
constraints: Compressed prompts remain derived projections; canonical authority and task capsules remain semantic owners
Token cost must be measured per tokenizer, never inferred from character count
Mandatory invariant hyperedges must remain complete
Symbolic and hypervector methods require baseline outperformance and held-out evaluation
No P0/P1/P2/P3 result promotes itself to P4
success: At least 50 percent median token reduction versus canonical prose on declared tokenizers
Zero critical invariant loss on adversarial benchmark cases
Non-inferior execution behavior established within a declared margin on available model runs
Exact-head repository tests and build pass
falsifiers: Any branch, worktree-preservation, authority, negation, exact-command, stop-condition, or evidence-boundary invariant is lost
Symbolic rendering increases token count or execution variance without compensating benefit
Hypervector retrieval fails to outperform deterministic diffusion or introduces authority drift
Compression/decompression overhead exceeds saved inference cost
The mathematical layer does not alter selection, proof obligations, or verification
research_inputs: Chung et al., Physical Review X 8, 031003 (2018)
